title: Crispy Ukrainian Beetroot Bowl
description: A kid-friendly, vegetarian bowl featuring roasted beetroot, fluffy quinoa, crunchy chickpeas, and a tangy gochujang glaze that delivers a satisfying sour kick.

ingredients.name: quinoa
ingredients.notes: rinse and cook in water until fluffy

instructions: Wash and trim the beets, then cut them into thin, bite‑size wedges.
Toss the beet wedges with 2 teaspoons of sesame oil, salt, and pepper; place them in the air fryer basket.
Air‑fry the beets for 12 minutes, shaking halfway through, until crisp and caramelized.
While the beets cook, rinse quinoa under cold water and cook in 2 cups of water until fluffy, about 15 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together gochujang, lime juice, soy sauce, minced garlic, and 1 tablespoon of sesame oil to create the sauce.
Heat a skillet over medium heat; add chickpeas and the gochujang sauce, cooking for 3–4 minutes until the chickpeas are heated through and coated.
Drain any excess sauce, then mix the chickpeas with the cooked quinoa; season lightly with salt and pepper.
To assemble, place a scoop of quinoa‑chickpea mixture in each bowl, then top with roasted beet wedges, cucumber slices, carrot sticks, a sprinkle of sesame seeds, and chopped cilantro.
Serve immediately, or chill for a bit if you prefer a cooler bite.
